sanskrit:
न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते।
न च सन्न्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति॥
transliteration:
na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣkarmyaṃ puruṣo ’śnute
na ca sannyasanād eva siddhiṃ samadhigacchati
literal_meaning: Not by non-performance of actions does a person enjoy actionlessness; nor by mere renunciation does he attain perfection.
contextual_meaning: Avoiding action or quitting responsibilities does not by itself free the mind from anxiety or produce maturity; genuine growth requires engaged, purified action.
psychological_state: Arjuna oscillates between fearful withdrawal and idealized escape, hoping to leapfrog inner work through external renunciation.
trigger_scenario: A burned-out professional contemplates quitting everything to 'find peace,' yet discovers that running away leaves the stress-circuitry intact.
anchor_text: True freedom comes through mindful action, not by avoiding duties or renouncing the world.
